fallback.description: Objects in {{path}}. Select an item in the tree or in the list.
path.root.title: Root
path.root.description: Root of the ISPF object tree. All platform and tenant namespaces hang under this node. Open root.platform for day-to-day administration or root.tenant for multi-tenant partitions.
path.root__platform.title: Platform
path.root__platform.description: Top-level platform namespace. Contains built-in catalogs (devices, dashboards, blueprints, automation, security, SQL objects) and user-created nodes. Select a child folder to manage that domain; edit metadata in the inspector or open specialized editors with a double-click.
path.root__tenant.title: Tenants
path.root__tenant.description: Multi-tenant namespace root. Each child tenant receives an isolated subtree root.tenant.{tenantId}.platform.* — devices, dashboards, and applications scoped to that tenant. Assign a tenantId to operator users in Security → Users to limit their visible tree.
path.root__platform__devices.title: Devices
path.root__platform__devices.description: Device catalog. Each DEVICE child runs a driver plugin (SNMP, Modbus, MQTT, virtual lab, JDBC, etc.) and exposes variables, events, and functions from its primary and Applied blueprints. Start/stop drivers from the Driver tab; bind variables on the Bindings tab. Use Federation bind (inspector) to proxy a remote device at a local path while keeping the operator path stable.
path.root__platform__mixin-blueprints.de: Mixin blueprints enrich existing objects with extra variables, events, functions, and binding rules without changing the object path. Create or edit BLUEPRINT children here, then attach blueprints to devices or folders via Applied blueprints in the inspector.
path.root__platform__instance-types.desc: Instance-type blueprints — templates for new object instances (devices, folders, custom nodes). When you create an object with a templateId, the platform materializes structure from the matching INSTANCE blueprint. Use Instance Types for repeatable device types; use Mixin Blueprints to add capabilities to existing nodes.
path.root__platform__singleton-blueprint: Singleton blueprints live as unique nodes under this catalog (root.platform.singleton-blueprints.*). Each child is both the blueprint and the live runtime object with application logic — variables, events, functions, and binding rules. Do not create duplicates.
path.root__platform__instances.title: Instances
path.root__platform__instances.descripti: User-created object instances — devices, meters, and other runtime objects (typically from Instance Types). Singleton Blueprints with application logic live under Singleton Blueprints, not here.
path.root__platform__dashboards.title: Dashboards
path.root__platform__dashboards.descript: HMI dashboard catalog. DASHBOARD objects store widget layouts for admin preview and operator mode. Widgets bind to device variables, sub-dashboards, SQL reports, maps, and workflows. Operator Apps reference dashboards by path; open the Dashboard editor with a double-click.
path.root__platform__reports.title: Reports
path.root__platform__reports.description: Platform-wide report catalog. REPORT children use report-v1 (SQL against a data source) or tree-variables-report-v1 (scan device variables by path pattern). Configure columns, parameters, and optional YARG export templates (CSV, HTML, XLSX, PDF). Application-owned reports may also live under root.platform.applications.{appId}.reports.
path.root__platform__data-sources.title: Data Sources
path.root__platform__data-sources.descri: PostgreSQL schema registry for SQL-backed platform features. Each DATA_SOURCE child maps a display name to a schema name (e.g. app_myapp) used by reports, SQL bindings, migrations, and script functions. Create manually in the Data Source editor or import from an application bundle deploy.
path.root__platform__schedules.title: Schedules
path.root__platform__schedules.descripti: Platform scheduler catalog (tree-first). Each SCHEDULE child runs on a fixed interval (intervalMs) and invokes a platform function. Jobs are persisted in the object tree and survive restarts. Application bundles may register schedules under root.platform.applications.{appId}.schedules.
path.root__platform__bindings.title: SQL Bindings
path.root__platform__bindings.descriptio: SQL-to-variable binding catalog. Each BINDING child runs a SQL statement against a registered data source on a poll interval and writes the result into target object variables. Used for bringing relational data into the live object model without custom drivers.
path.root__platform__migrations.title: Migrations
path.root__platform__migrations.descript: SQL migration script catalog. Each MIGRATION child stores DDL/DML applied to a data source schema, typically during bundle import. The Migration editor tracks checksum, apply state, and history. Platform migrations live here; app-specific scripts may also appear under applications.{appId}.migrations.
path.root__platform__workflows.title: Workflows
path.root__platform__workflows.descripti: BPMN 2.0 workflow catalog. WORKFLOW children define automation processes integrated with NATS messaging, device functions, timers, and user tasks. Activate workflows from the Workflow editor; link an Operator App so user tasks appear in the operator sidebar Work queue. Correlators can start workflows when event patterns match.
path.root__platform__alert-rules.title: Alert Rules
path.root__platform__alert-rules.descrip: CEL alert rule catalog. Each ALERT child watches variable changes on a target object and publishes an event when the CEL condition is true (optionally edge-triggered to fire only on false→true transitions). Use alert rules to turn telemetry into discrete alarm events consumed by correlators, workflows, or dashboards.
path.root__platform__correlators.title: Correlators
path.root__platform__correlators.descrip: Event correlator catalog. Each CORRELATOR child matches event patterns across objects — COUNT (event A then B within a window) or SEQUENCE (ordered chain) — and triggers a workflow or publishes a follow-up event. Place correlators between alert rules and BPMN automation.
path.root__platform__applications.title: Applications
path.root__platform__applications.descri: Deployed application bundle containers. Each APPLICATION child (folder name = appId / packageId) holds standard subfolders: functions, reports, schedules, bindings, migrations, and optional screens. Import packages via Explorer deploy, AI Studio bundle publish, or REST deploy API. Bundle content is expanded into both root.platform.applications.{appId}.* and shared platform catalogs where applicable.
path.root__platform__operator-apps.title: Operator Apps
path.root__platform__operator-apps.descr: Operator HMI application registry. Each OPERATOR_APP child defines the operator shell: title, dashboard and report navigation, optional alarm bar rules, and work queue scope for URL ?mode=operator&app={appId}. Unlike APPLICATION bundles, operator apps reference existing DASHBOARD/REPORT paths in the tree — they do not embed layout JSON. Configure from Explorer → Operator Apps or create via + Operator app.
path.root__platform__federation.title: Federation
path.root__platform__federation.descript: Federation hub for remote ISPF instances. Object path and service endpoint are separate concerns: register each peer's base URL in System → Federation → Peers (this tree folder holds catalog mirrors, not peer credentials). For loopback or tunnel testing, add a peer pointing at the tunnel endpoint and leave auth empty when using registration codes; for production remote sites, set authToken to a service account Bearer or use Get token / issue token on the Tokens tab. Catalog sync imports the remote object list under root.platform.federation.{peerName}.* as read-only mirrors. Federation bind (object inspector) overlays a remote object onto any local path you choose for production HMI and drivers. Mirror nodes here are for discovery — use Place locally or bind to adopt remote data at operator-facing paths.
path.root__platform__security.title: Security
path.root__platform__security.descriptio: Platform authentication and RBAC root. Contains Users and Roles folders synchronized with the security store. Manage accounts via System → Security panels or REST /api/v1/security/*. OIDC mode delegates login to Keycloak; local users remain in the tree for role assignment and auto-start operator settings.
path.root__platform__security__users.des: Platform user account folder. Each USER child mirrors one login: username, displayName, role list, enabled flag, and operator auto-start (open a specific Operator App after login). Admin users see the full admin console; operator users land in operator mode. CRUD via Security → Users or POST/PUT/DELETE /api/v1/security/users (admin role). Deleting a USER node removes the backing account.
path.root__platform__security__roles.des: RBAC role definitions. Each ROLE child stores roleName and description; users reference roles by name in their roles variable. Built-in admin and operator roles are seeded at first startup. Custom roles extend authorization checks across REST, WebSocket, and UI features.
path.root__platform__mimics.title: Mimics
path.root__platform__mimics.description: SCADA mimic diagram catalog. Each MIMIC child stores a process schematic (tanks, valves, pipelines) for operator HMI. Create mimics here, then embed them in dashboards or open the Mimic editor.
pattern.applicationRoot.description: Application bundle root for this appId. Deploy imports populate child folders: functions (callable API handlers), reports, schedules, bindings, migrations, and optional legacy screens. The appId equals the package folder name and is used in deploy REST paths and SQL schema defaults.
pattern.applicationFunctions.title: Functions
pattern.applicationFunctions.description: Callable functions owned by this application bundle. Each FUNCTION child is invoked via POST /api/v1/objects/by-path/.../functions/{name}/invoke, workflows, schedules, or dashboard widgets. Implementations may be script, nested platform function, or JDBC against the app's data source.
pattern.applicationReports.title: Reports
pattern.applicationReports.description: SQL and tree reports shipped with this application. Same report-v1 semantics as root.platform.reports but scoped to the app deploy. Data sources typically resolve to root.platform.data-sources.{appId}.
pattern.applicationSchedules.title: Schedules
pattern.applicationSchedules.description: Interval jobs registered by this application bundle. Each SCHEDULE invokes an app function every intervalMs milliseconds.
pattern.applicationBindings.title: SQL Bindings
pattern.applicationBindings.description: SQL-to-variable bindings owned by this application. Poll SQL against the app schema and write into object variables.
pattern.applicationMigrations.title: Migrations
pattern.applicationMigrations.descriptio: Database migrations shipped with this application. Applied automatically on bundle import or manually from the Migration editor.
pattern.applicationScreens.title: Screens
pattern.applicationScreens.description: Legacy operator manifest screens from older bundle formats. Prefer Operator Apps + dashboards for new HMI work.
pattern.operatorApp.description: Operator HMI entry for this appId. Variables define title, ordered dashboard/report lists, alarm bar configuration, and workflow user-task scope. Open operator mode with ?mode=operator&app={appId}. Does not contain widget JSON — it points at DASHBOARD and REPORT paths elsewhere in the tree.
pattern.federationPeer.description: Catalog mirror for federation peer "{{peerName}}". Child nodes are read-only proxies of objects on the remote ISPF site (devices, dashboards, workflows, etc.). Use Federation → Sync catalog to refresh. To use remote data in production HMI, either Place locally… from a mirror node or Federation bind onto an existing local path in the inspector.
